title: Global Head of Fertiliser Trading & Strategy
description:
Job Summary:

  • Basic Salary: $18000 - $22000

  • Location: Somerset

  • Working hours: Monday - Friday 9am - 6pm ; Alternate Sat 9am - 1pmAWS + Variable Bonus

Job Responsibilities:
Strategy & Portfolio

  • Define and execute the global fertiliser trading strategy aligned with growth objectives

  • Develop a multi-year roadmap covering priority products, markets, and trade corridors

  • Build a diversified portfolio across urea, sulphur, and related fertilisers

Market Intelligence

  • Lead market analysis on supply-demand, crop cycles, geopolitics, and regulations

  • Anticipate demand shifts driven by food security, climate, and sustainability trends

Trade Origination & Execution

  • Originate, negotiate, and execute physical fertiliser trades

  • Manage the full trade lifecycle from structuring and pricing to delivery

  • Optimise regional arbitrage with Singapore as the trading hub

P&L & Risk

  • Own fertiliser trading P&L, ensuring margin, profitability, and capital discipline

  • Partner with risk teams on limits, credit exposure, and hedging strategies

Counterparty & Stakeholder Management

  • Build senior-level relationships with producers, buyers, distributors, and governments

  • Expand trade lines and represent the firm at global industry forums

Execution, Governance & Team

  • Ensure compliant trade execution with operations, finance, and legal teams

  • Build and mentor a high-performance fertiliser trading team

  • Deliver business plans, performance reporting, and strategic updates to management

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Agriculture, Economics, Business, Engineering, or a related discipline

  • 10–15 years of experience in fertiliser or agricultural commodities trading

  • Proven track record of building and managing global fertiliser trading books from a major trading hub

  • Strong expertise in global fertiliser markets, including pricing, logistics, and agriculture-driven demand dynamics

  • Solid understanding of physical trading, shipping, and international trade contracts

  • Strategic and commercially driven, able to balance short-term trading performance with long-term portfolio growth

  • Entrepreneurial and self-motivated, comfortable operating in a build-out or growth environment

  • Strong leadership, negotiation, and senior stakeholder management capabilities

  • Able to operate effectively in a multicultural, international setting, based in Singapore

Applicants who possess relevant experience for the above responsibilities are most welcome to apply. If you do not possess the above experience, your application will still be considered on individual merits and you may be contacted for other opportunities.

Please submit your updated resume in MS Words format by using the APPLY NOW BUTTON.

By submitting your personal data and/or resume, you give consent to collection, use and disclosure of your personal data and/or resume by the company (or its agent) for the purpose of the processing and administration by the company relating to this job application.

https://www.mci.com.sg/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/MCI-Job-Applicant-Data-Protection-Notice.pdf

**We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates would be notified.
 

Foo Kai Jing (Fiona)
